message:
fix(clippy): resolve warnings across 15 crates — lifetime elision, deprecated APIs, idiomatic patterns

Systematic clippy lint fixes touching frankenterm-core, client, deps-freetype,
deps-harfbuzz, frecency, lfucache, mux-lua, open-url, tabout, and
toast-notification:

Lifetime elision (clippy::needless_lifetimes):
- Remove unused 'a lifetime parameter from MuxDomain::resolve,
  MuxPane::resolve, and MuxTab::resolve in mux-lua — the lifetime was
  declared on the &'a Arc<Mux> parameter but never used in the return type

Deprecated API replacements:
- usize::max_value() → usize::MAX in pane.rs (deprecated since Rust 1.53)
- std::io::Error::new(ErrorKind::Other, ...) → std::io::Error::other(..)
  in tabout/src/lib.rs (stabilized in Rust 1.74)

Idiomatic pattern improvements:
- match { Variant(t) => {..} _ => {} } → if let Variant(t) = .. in tab.rs
  (clippy::single_match / irrefutable_let_patterns)
- *freq = *freq / delta → *freq /= delta in lfucache (clippy::assign_op_pattern)
- Manual hasher build (build_hasher + hash + finish) → hash_one() in lfucache
  (cleaner BuildHasher API, also removes unused Hasher import)
- Octal literal 08 → 8 in frecency test (clippy::zero_prefixed_literal)
- Unnecessary &* deref-ref patterns removed in toast-notification/macos.rs

Structural fixes:
- Added LfuCache::is_empty() method (clippy::len_without_is_empty)
- Moved ?Sized bound from generic param to where clause on LfuCache::get
- Replaced LazyLock<Retained<UNUserNotificationCenter>> const with
  get_center() function in toast-notification — avoids thread-safety issues
  with the ObjC notification center singleton being captured in a static
- Simplified open-url early-return logic: the success check was unreachable
  (spawned thread, return already happened), replaced with let _ = cmd.status()

Also updates .beads/issues.jsonl with current issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-3681t.5.8","title":"[connectors] Multi-host connector mesh federation","description":"Integrate mesh capabilities for running connector workloads across multiple hosts/zones while preserving policy, observability, and failure containment.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Mesh routing and tenancy boundaries are explicit. 2. Cross-host behavior is resilient to partial outages. 3. Control-plane visibility remains consistent across hosts.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OliveHarbor","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:13:43.720804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-04T04:42:33.597187Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T04:35:31.799664Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","mesh","multi-host"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:09.171426Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:13:43.720804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:08.568078Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1844,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.8","author":"OliveHarbor","text":"Claiming this bead.  {"id":"ft-3681t.6","title":"[epic] Unified policy, security, and governance","description":"# Epic 6: Unified Security, Policy, and Governance

Merge mux/orchestration controls and connector permissions into one enforceable governance model with approvals, revocation, and forensic traceability.","acceptance_criteria":"1. One policy model governs terminal actions, workflows, and connector capabilities.
2. Approval and revocation flows are enforceable and auditable.
3. Forensics/export cover operational and compliance needs.

Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. Also fixed upstream compile blockers in resize_scheduler and hardened bench cfg in benches/tui_rendering for all-target checks. Validation via rch wrappers: workspace check/clippy/fmt are currently blocked by pre-existing unrelated workspace issues (frankenterm-gui unresolved k9/benchmarking in shapecache.rs; clippy cargo-common-metadata on tabout/window; widespread pre-existing fmt diffs). Focused gate cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets passes (warnings only).","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:00:37Z"},{"id":1835,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Progress update (HazyBarn, 2026-03-03/04): tightened policy surface attribution for mux mutation paths. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs, PolicyEngine::check_send compatibility shim and PolicyGatedInjector::inject now explicitly set PolicySurface::Mux on PolicyInput before authorize(). Added regressions: check_send_compatibility_path_uses_mux_surface and injector_policy_context_marks_mux_surface. Validation (required via rch wrappers): rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-hazybarn-ft3681t61-policysurface cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib (pass); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-hazybarn-ft3681t61-policysurface cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib check_send_compatibility_path_uses_mux_surface -- --nocapture (pass); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-hazybarn-ft3681t61-policysurface cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib injector_policy_context_marks_mux_surface -- --nocapture (pass). Note: rch remote SSH failed in these runs and fell back to local execution.","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:32:35Z"},{"id":1839,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Follow-up slice (HazyBarn): explicit policy surface tagging added in MCP tool handlers to increase cross-surface traceability. Updated crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs policy inputs with with_surface(...) mappings: ReadOutput/SearchOutput/SendText -> mux, WorkflowRun -> workflow, ReservePane/ReleasePane -> swarm. Added missing import crate::policy::PolicySurface. Validation (via rch wrappers): rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-hazybarn-ft3681t61-policysurface cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib (pass after import fix); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-hazybarn-ft3681t61-policysurface cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib mcp_tools::tests::tool_count_is_29 -- --nocapture (pass). Remote worker SSH failed; rch failed open to local execution.","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:40:00Z"},{"id":1873,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"GoldHollow","text":"GoldHollow starting focused slice: finish explicit PolicySurface attribution in production CLI policy call sites in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs (read/search/send/prepare/commit paths). I have exclusive reservation on crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and will run targeted rch-offloaded validation before posting results.","created_at":"2026-03-04T06:39:26Z"},{"id":1876,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"CrimsonForest verification slice (2026-03-04): during execution, concurrent commit f7d341e4 landed policy surface regressions in crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs (tests: policy_rules_match_exec_command_surface_case_insensitive, explicit_surface_override_takes_precedence_over_actor_default). I independently validated via rch wrappers: rch exec -- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs (pass); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-crimsonforest-ft3681t61-policy cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib (pass); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-crimsonforest-ft3681t61-policy cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib policy_rules_match_exec_command_surface_case_insensitive -- --nocapture (pass); rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-crimsonforest-ft3681t61-policy cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib explicit_surface_override_takes_precedence_over_actor_default -- --nocapture (pass). rch remote execution failed open to local in this environment.","created_at":"2026-03-04T07:09:46Z"},{"id":1878,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"SwiftSpring","text":"Implemented policy-surface scoping for allow-once approval fingerprints in crates/frankenterm-core/src/approval.rs. Added regression coverage in approval_labruntime (approval_different_policy_surface_prevents_consumption), updated in-crate fingerprint tests, and added proptest fingerprint_surface_sensitivity coverage. Targeted tests pass via rch wrapper (fallback local): approval_different_policy_surface_prevents_consumption, approval::tests::fingerprint_surface_changes_hash, proptest_approval::fingerprint_surface_sensitivity. Workspace-wide cargo check/clippy currently fail due pre-existing unrelated issues in frankenterm-gui and other crates (missing k9/benchmarking symbols and clippy metadata/style violations).","created_at":"2026-03-04T07:36:56Z"},{"id":1884,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"GoldBear","text":"GoldBear support slice (2026-03-04) for ft-3681t.6.1 in `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`.

Scope:
- Strengthened dry-run policy traceability by surfacing explicit policy-surface evidence in send dry-run evaluation.

Code changes:
1) In `build_send_dry_run_report(...)`, after `engine.authorize(&input)`:
   - Added a `PolicyCheck` entry named `policy_surface` with message `Policy surface: <surface>` when decision context is present.
   - Added fallback `policy_surface` check with `Policy surface: unknown (context unavailable)` when context is absent.
2) Added regression test `send_dry_run_report_includes_mux_policy_surface_check` verifying send dry-run reports include `policy_surface` and that explicit surface tagging resolves to `mux`.

Validation (CPU-heavy commands via `rch exec`):
1) `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-goldbear-ft3681t61-main cargo test -p frankenterm --bin ft send_dry_run_report_includes_mux_policy_surface_check -- --nocapture` -> PASS
2) `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-goldbear-ft3681t61-main cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` -> PASS
3) `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-goldbear-ft3681t61-main cargo clippy -p frankenterm --bin ft -- -D warnings` -> FAIL on pre-existing unrelated Cargo metadata lint errors (`tabout`/`window` missing readme/keywords/categories)
4) `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` -> PASS

Infra note:
- `rch` repeatedly attempted remote workers first, then failed open to local execution due worker SSH/connectivity errors in this environment.
","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:13:52Z"},{"id":1888,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"SunnyBeaver","text":"Support slice (SunnyBeaver, 2026-03-05): added MCP SearchOutput policy-surface regression coverage in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs via tests search_policy_mcp_actor_uses_mux_surface_override + search_policy_mcp_actor_matches_mux_surface_rule. Confirms authorize_read_or_search_policy keeps PolicySurface::Mux for MCP search paths and matches mux-scoped deny rules while bypassing mcp-scoped surface rules. Validation via rch wrappers: (1) CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-sunnybeaver-ft3681t61-search rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm --bin ft search_policy_mcp_actor_uses_mux_surface_override -- --nocapture (pass), (2) ... search_policy_mcp_actor_matches_mux_surface_rule ... (pass), (3) rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft (pass, warnings only). rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs (pass). Targeted clippy remains blocked by pre-existing cargo metadata lint failures in tabout/window under -D warnings. rch remote SSH failed-open to local execution in this environment.","created_at":"2026-03-05T01:23:13Z"}]}

Workspace-wide cargo check/clippy currently fail due pre-existing unrelated issues in frankenterm-gui and other crates (missing k9/benchmarking symbols and clippy metadata/style violations).","created_at":"2026-03-04T07:36:56Z"},{"id":1884,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"GoldBear","text":"GoldBear support slice (2026-03-04) for ft-3681t.6.1 in `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`.

Scope:
- Strengthened dry-run policy traceability by surfacing explicit policy-surface evidence in send dry-run evaluation.

Code changes:
1) In `build_send_dry_run_report(...)`, after `engine.authorize(&input)`:
   - Added a `PolicyCheck` entry named `policy_surface` with message `Policy surface: <surface>` when decision context is present.
   - Added fallback `policy_surface` check with `Policy surface: unknown (context unavailable)` when context is absent.
2) Added regression test `send_dry_run_report_includes_mux_policy_surface_check` verifying send dry-run reports include `policy_surface` and that explicit surface tagging resolves to `mux`.

Validation (CPU-heavy commands via `rch exec`):
1) `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-goldbear-ft3681t61-main cargo test -p frankenterm --bin ft send_dry_run_report_includes_mux_policy_surface_check -- --nocapture` -> PASS
2) `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-goldbear-ft3681t61-main cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` -> PASS
3) `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-goldbear-ft3681t61-main cargo clippy -p frankenterm --bin ft -- -D warnings` -> FAIL on pre-existing unrelated Cargo metadata lint errors (`tabout`/`window` missing readme/keywords/categories)
4) `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` -> PASS

Infra note:
- `rch` repeatedly attempted remote workers first, then failed open to local execution due worker SSH/connectivity errors in this environment.

Targeted rch cargo test for new helper test (timeouts 300s and 180s) failed to produce green artifact due remote SSH fail-open to local and long compile timing out.","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:05:59Z"},{"id":1891,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"BronzeCove support slice (2026-03-05): cleaned custom-policy rule trace bookkeeping in crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs to avoid duplicate selected-rule entries and surface matched rule IDs explicitly.

Code changes:
1) RuleEvaluationResult now includes matched_rule_ids and evaluate_policy_rules populates it.
2) PolicyEngine::authorize custom-rule trace path now records each config.rule.* entry with matched/decision/reason in one place.
3) Removed duplicate record_rule calls in deny/require_approval/allow branches for selected custom rule.
4) Added/updated tests: policy_rules_disabled_config_matches_nothing, policy_rules_precedence_priority_wins, policy_rules_integrated_into_authorize_deny, rule_evaluation_result_debug_clone.

Validation (CPU-heavy cargo commands via rch):
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-bronzecove-ft3681t61-policy cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib -> PASS (warnings only: existing dead_code in policy.rs + mux_client.rs).
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-bronzecove-ft3681t61-policy cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib policy_rules_ -- --nocapture -> PASS (19 tests).
- rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-rch-bronzecove-ft3681t61-policy cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib rule_evaluation_result_debug_clone -- --nocapture -> BLOCKED by unrelated concurrent compile errors in crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs (input_text not found, E0425), outside this policy.rs slice.

Coordination:
- Replied to TopazReef in thread ft-3681t.6.1 confirming E0631 fix at policy.rs:3171 is already folded into this slice.
- Reservation for crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs currently still held by BronzeCove; can release immediately after coordinator confirms no further policy.rs handoff needed.","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:10:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.6.2","title":"[policy] Identity graph and least-privilege authorization model","description":"Define identity and authorization mapping across agent identities, panes/sessions, workflows, connectors, and hosts with least-privilege defaults and explicit delegation rules.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Identity graph resolves principal/action/resource consistently. 2. Delegation and scoping rules are explicit. 3. Authorization errors are understandable and actionable.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5.
